methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Alex Tseng and Devan Plantamura. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of ten comparable companies—two public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private entities including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Anduril Industries, and Saronic—all operating in the Defense & Security sector at equivalent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using the company's last post-money valuation of $150M and applying a typical Series A–to–next–stage multiplier of 2.0x, implies a valuation of $300M. The peer-median approach examines three directly comparable same-sector, same-stage companies, yielding a median valuation of $500M across a range of $300M to $29.0B. The wide range reflects heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the inclusion of Scale AI at $29.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a defensible range of $255M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $400M across the two methods. The current $150M post-money sits materially below this range, suggesting upside potential. However, material uncertainty remains around peer comparability—particularly the valuation dispersion in the Defense & Security sector—and the early-stage nature of the business limits visibility into revenue trajectory and unit economics. Further diligence on growth rates and path to profitability would refine this estimate.
